type: content/directory site (dividend-investing tools directory; affiliate + sponsorship monetization)
asking_price: $5,000 (TrustMRR; '0.1x' = one-time-heavy 30d revenue annualized (~$73.7k implied ARR) — honest recurring multiple: 2.35x on $177 MRR; verify_figures flagged the 24x gap)
one_line_reason: A dividend-tools directory whose $21k headline revenue is ~97% one-time sponsorship/listing sales driven by the founder's 'Top Dividend ETFs' X audience — a content-and-ad-sales media business (the team's declared weakness category), with $177 of actual recurring MRR, selling for less than one month's peak revenue, which is itself the tell.

operability_upside.note: There is no meaningful software: a directory page with Stripe. Operating it means curating listings daily, selling sponsorships to fintech advertisers, and feeding a finance content audience — a content treadmill plus ad-sales job. The one dimension where the team's engineering strength buys nothing.

pmf_retention.note: Advertiser demand was real ($21,364 in 7 months) but spiky and one-time: $4,164 of the last 30d's $6,138 landed in one week; 30d revenue growth is already negative (-1.2%). Recurring base: 14 subs, $177 MRR. No evidence sponsors renew.

unit_economics.note: 97% margin — directories are pure margin. But the economics are one-time listing sales; LTV = one purchase per advertiser until proven otherwise. The recurring layer ($12.64 ARPU × 14) is a rounding error.

moat_defensibility.note: DR 22 (best in TrustMRR batch), a niche brand, first-mover claim. But a directory is a weekend replica, and the real moat — the Top Dividend ETFs X audience — may not even be part of the sale. AI answers ('best dividend tracker?') are eating the directory format.

growth_market.note: Retail dividend-investing interest is evergreen and growing, but directory/affiliate sites as a format are in structural decline as LLMs answer recommendation queries directly.

tech_ip.note: Trivial, transferable site (domain + content + Stripe). Nothing to break; also nothing proprietary. The IP question is the X account and advertiser relationships, not code.

transfer_key_person.note: The founder IS the media property — listed on TrustMRR as 'Top Dividend ETFs (9,602 X followers)', with the site's contact at Business@topdividendETFs.com. Traffic and advertiser demand flow from that persona. DR-22 SEO transfers; the audience and DM-based ad pipeline likely don't.

deal_terms.note: $5,000 is 0.23x all-time / 2.35x recurring — and less than ONE month of claimed peak revenue. If $6k/mo with 'HUGE advertiser demand' were durable, no rational seller takes $5k. The price prices the truth: the spike doesn't repeat. Also 1.67x the budget, 15 competing offers.

seller_adverse_selection.note: Classic lemon dressing: hype message ('HUGE advertiser demand!'), a 0.1x badge built on annualizing one-time sales, sale price below one month's headline revenue, revenue growth already negative, and the audience-owner exiting while keeping (presumably) the X account that generates the demand. The insider's price contradicts the insider's pitch.

key_unknowns: Revenue composition — exactly how much of the $21,364 is one-time listing/sponsorship sales vs anything recurring; Stripe export would show it in minutes. The 0.1x-vs-2.35x gap says ~97% one-time.

Whether the 'Top Dividend ETFs' X account (9,602 followers) is included in the sale — it is the traffic and advertiser-demand engine; without it the buyer gets a directory with DR 22 and no megaphone.
Traffic breakdown: X referrals vs organic search vs direct — determines what survives the founder's exit.
Advertiser repeat behavior: have ANY sponsors bought twice? A renewal history would be the one genuinely bullish fact.
What the 14 subscriptions ($177 MRR) actually are — premium listings? A newsletter? — and their churn.
Whether the -1.2% 30d revenue growth marks the start of the decline the seller is front-running.
seller_questions: Share a read-only Stripe export split by product: one-time listing/sponsorship sales vs recurring subscriptions, by month since December. — resolves: revenue_evidence → buyer_verified / pmf; why: $177 MRR vs $6,138 last-30d says ~97% of revenue is one-time — we need the exact split and trend.

Is the 'Top Dividend ETFs' X account (9,602 followers) included in the sale? If not, what % of site traffic and advertiser inquiries come from it? Share analytics (source breakdown, last 90 days). — resolves: value_transfers / not_fragile; why: the audience appears to be the entire demand engine.

List every advertiser/sponsor to date with what they bought and whether any has purchased more than once. Any active contracts or committed renewals that transfer? — resolves: pmf_retention / unit_economics; why: one-time sales are a spent asset; renewals are a business.
What are the 14 active subscriptions ($177 MRR) — premium listings, newsletter, something else — and their cancellation history? — resolves: unit_economics.

Why sell for $5,000 — less than one month of your claimed revenue — if advertiser demand is 'HUGE'? What changed in the last 30 days (revenue growth is -1.2%)? — resolves: seller_adverse_selection; why: the price contradicts the pitch, and the honest answer is the deal.

Who writes/updates the directory ('Updated Daily') and how many hours weekly does curation + sponsor sales take? — resolves: operability; why: the buyer needs to know the size of the content-and-sales job they'd inherit.

Given the buyer's $3,000 ceiling: any price flexibility or an earnout tied to sponsor revenue over the next 6 months? — resolves: deal_terms / affordability; why: an earnout would also test the seller's confidence in the 'huge demand' claim.
inputs_provided: Listing URL trustmrr.com/startup/topdividendtools (user) — VERIFIED as existing

TrustMRR .md profile: $21,364 all-time (7 months), $6,138 last-30d (-1.2%), $4,164 last-7d, $12.95 last-24h, $177 MRR (+17.1%), 14 subs, 97% margin, ~1,000 users, DR 22.0, $5,000 asking, '0.1x', 1,275 interested, 15 offers, founded 2025-12-11, US, founder 'Top Dividend ETFs (9,602 X followers)', pricing '100% FREE', Stripe-verified badge, seller message 'HUGE advertiser demand!' — MARKETPLACE_ATTESTED

Live site topdividendtools.com: directory with filterable categories, affiliate links (disclosed), sponsored banner (ETF provider), paid 'submit tool' option, contact Business@topdividendETFs.com, 'Seen by thousands monthly' — VERIFIED (live site)

Multiple reconciliation: stated 0.1x = 30d revenue annualized (~$73.7k); recurring multiple = 2.35x on $177 MRR; verify_figures flagged 24x gap — VERIFIED arithmetic; gap = revenue composition, not data error
Business type: content/directory/media — flagged per skill Phase 1 as sitting against the team's weakness regardless of other merits
Budget ceiling $3,000 / no financing — reused from session convention

sources.title: TopDividendTools live site (monetization: affiliate + sponsored banners + paid submissions)

scorecard_markdown:
# Acquisition Scorecard (v2) — TopDividendTools (topdividendtools.com)

**Type:** content/directory site (dividend-investing tools; affiliate + sponsorship monetization)  ·  **Asking:** $5,000 ('0.1x' badge = one-time-heavy 30d revenue annualized; honest recurring multiple 2.35x)  ·  **Budget:** $3,000  ·  **Date:** 2026-07-18
**Figures reconciled?** verify_figures ⚠ flagged a **24x gap** between stated (0.1x) and recurring (2.35x) multiples → reconciled: ~97% of headline revenue is one-time sponsorship/listing sales; $177 MRR is the recurring truth · revenue evidence: marketplace_attested (Stripe badge)
**Sources reviewed:** TrustMRR .md profile, topdividendtools.com (monetization model)

## Verdict

> **PASS (provisional) — 37/100, OUT OF BUDGET** (completeness 66%)
> This is not a software business — it's a **media property**: a directory monetized by one-time sponsorship and listing sales, powered by the founder's 'Top Dividend ETFs' X audience (9,602 followers), which may not even be part of the sale. The skill's Phase-1 rule fires on classification alone: content/directory sites are marketing-and-channel businesses that sit against the team's declared weakness regardless of other merits. The numbers confirm it: of $21,364 all-time, only $177/mo recurs; $4,164 of the last month landed in one week; 30-day growth is already negative. And the adverse-selection logic is airtight — **nobody sells a '$6k/month, HUGE demand' asset for $5,000, less than one month's revenue,** unless they know the spike is spent. Verified kill gate: growth_lever_fits=false (the business IS content + audience + ad sales). $5,000 is 1.67x the ceiling, 15 offers in. Biggest swing factor: none that helps — a Stripe export showing sponsor renewals would raise merit a few points but can't change what the business is.

## Scorecard (nine dimensions)

| # | Dimension | Wt | Score /5 | Conf | Weighted | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Operability & product upside | 15 | 1 | high | 3.0 | No software to improve; operating it = daily curation + sponsor sales + finance content. A content treadmill, verbatim from the team's avoid-list. |
| 2 | PMF & retention | 15 | 2 | med | 6.0 | Real advertiser demand happened once; no renewal evidence; -1.2% 30d growth; $177 MRR recurring. |
| 3 | Unit economics & upside | 12 | 2 | med | 4.8 | 97% margin on one-time sales; LTV = one purchase until proven otherwise. |
| 4 | Moat & defensibility | 12 | 2 | med | 4.8 | DR 22 (batch best) + niche brand; but directories are weekend replicas and LLMs are eating the format. |
| 5 | Growth & market | 8 | 2 | med | 3.2 | Dividend niche evergreen; directory/affiliate format structurally declining. |
| 6 | Tech & IP health | 8 | 3 | med | 4.8 | Trivial transferable site; the real IP (X account, advertiser relationships) is the open question. |
| 7 | Transfer / key-person | 10 | 2 | med | 4.0 | The founder IS the media property; DR-22 SEO transfers, the audience and DM ad-pipeline likely don't. |
| 8 | Deal terms & value | 10 | 2 | med | 4.0 | $5k = less than one month's claimed revenue — the price confesses what the pitch denies. 1.67x budget. |
| 9 | Seller & adverse-selection | 10 | 1 | med | 2.0 | Hype message + spike-built badge + below-one-month price + negative growth + audience likely retained = lemon dressing. |
| | **Merit total** | **100** | | | **36.6** | |

**Gates:** operable ✓ · growth-lever-fits ✗ (caps → PASS) · revenue-evidence marketplace_attested (provisional) · value-transfers ? · IP-transferable ? · not-fragile ? · **affordable** NO ($5,000 vs $3,000 → OUT OF BUDGET)

## Fit-to-build read (the team's lens)

There is nothing to build. The 'product' is a filterable table; the business is publishing cadence, audience growth on X, and selling banner spots to fintech marketers. Every hour an owner puts in is a content-and-sales hour. This is the single worst fit-to-build in the entire deal corpus — worse than the SaaS PASSes, because those at least contained software.

## Does the value transfer? (key-person check)

The demand engine is a named X persona ('Top Dividend ETFs') whose email domain even hosts the site's business contact. If the account conveys, the buyer inherits a content treadmill they can't feed; if it doesn't, they buy a DR-22 domain and a table of links. Either branch fails the team.

## What we know vs. what we're assuming

- **Verified (buyer-reconciled):** the 24x gap between badge multiple and recurring multiple; the one-week $4,164 revenue burst; negative 30d growth; the monetization model (affiliate + sponsorships + paid submissions); the X-brand linkage; business-type classification (content/media).
- **Assumed / marketplace-attested:** all dollar figures (Stripe badge); 14 subs; ~1,000 users; DR 22.
- **Unknown (and how it swings):** revenue composition by product (Stripe export — expected ~97% one-time); X account inclusion; traffic sources; sponsor renewals (the only possible upside surprise); what the 14 subs are. None of it flips the classification gate.

## Seller questions to send (prioritized)

1. Read-only Stripe export split one-time vs recurring, by month. — *resolves: revenue → buyer_verified; the 0.1x-vs-2.35x gap says it all — show the split.*
2. Is the Top Dividend ETFs X account included? What % of traffic/advertiser inquiries come from it? Analytics for 90 days. — *resolves: value_transfers / not_fragile.*
3. Every sponsor to date: what they bought, and has anyone bought twice? Any contracts that transfer? — *resolves: pmf / unit_economics.*
4. What are the 14 subscriptions and their churn? — *resolves: unit_economics.*
5. Why $5,000 — under one month's claimed revenue — if demand is 'HUGE'? What changed in the last 30 days? — *resolves: adverse-selection.*
6. Who does the daily curation and sponsor sales, and how many hours weekly? — *resolves: operability.*
7. Price flexibility to $3,000, or an earnout on 6-month sponsor revenue? — *resolves: affordability; also tests the seller's own confidence.*

## Recommended next step

**Pass — wrong business type, full stop.** Even if every number verifies, this is a media/ad-sales operation the team has explicitly chosen not to be. The corpus now has its cleanest illustration of the marketplace pattern: headline revenue built from one-time spikes, a badge multiple that annualizes them, and a seller price that quietly tells the truth the pitch won't.
